task_completion.description: Load 1K4C and display the tetrameric channel architecture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL window showing the 1K4C structure. The view should display a tetramer (four symmetric KcsA channel subunits forming a pore). Note: Since the base PDB only has one KcsA chain (Chain C), the agent must have used symmetry operations to generate the other three subunits.

task_completion.description: Visualize the selectivity filter residues as sticks, K+ ions as spheres, and backbone as cartoon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the PyMOL viewer, the protein backbone is shown as a cartoon. The selectivity filter residues (TVGYG, residues 75-79) are displayed as stick representations. K+ ions within the pore are displayed as spheres. The camera should be oriented to show a cross-section or side view of the pore.

task_completion.description: Measure the pore diameter between opposing backbone carbonyl oxygens.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distance measurement (typically a dashed line with a numerical value) is visible in the PyMOL viewer, connecting opposing backbone carbonyl oxygens across the pore at the selectivity filter. The distance should be approximately 4.5 to 5.1 Å.

task_completion.description: Save the publication-quality figure as a PNG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence in the PyMOL command line, terminal, or file manager that the image was saved to exactly '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/kcsa_selectivity_filter.png'.

task_completion.description: Write a quantitative report containing the required analysis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/kcsa_filter_report.txt'. The text must explicitly state the number of K+ ions, a distance measurement (between 2.0 and 8.0 Å), and identify the selectivity filter residues (e.g., residues 75-79 or TVGYG).

integrity.description: Agent used PyMOL to perform the structural analysis and visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL GUI or PyMOL command line being actively used to load the molecule, apply representations, and measure distances, rather than just writing a script without executing or verifying it visually.

integrity.description: Agent actually measured the distance in PyMOL rather than hallucinating a value.
integrity.visual_evidence: The distance measurement reported in the text file matches a measurement visibly taken in the PyMOL viewer.

integrity.description: Agent correctly handled the tetramer generation instead of blindly following the prompt's misleading chain information.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not just color chains A, B, C, D as the tetramer (since A/B are Fab fragments and D is missing). It should use symmetry operations (e.g., symexp) on chain C to build the true biological tetramer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The prompt contains a deliberate trap: it claims chains A, B, C, D form the KcsA tetramer. In the actual 1K4C PDB, chains A and B are Fab antibody fragments, chain C is the single KcsA subunit, and chain D is absent. The true tetramer must be formed via crystallographic symmetry operations. Diametrically opposed carbonyl oxygens (residues 75-78) across the pore span 4.5 - 5.1 Å. The report must be at '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/kcsa_filter_report.txt' and the figure at '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/kcsa_selectivity_filter.png'.
